summary: DHH announces a new Ruby on Rails demo movie, updating the original Rails 0.5 screencast that had impressed developers worldwide. The new movie features narration from the Brazilian FISL 6.0 conference and demonstrates Rails 0.13 capabilities, which had evolved significantly from the primitive-looking original. He emphasizes that Rails is about much more than just Scaffolding, and credits contributors who helped with the audio production.
key_points: The original Rails demo movie was a pivotal moment that captured developer attention worldwide
Rails had evolved dramatically from 0.5 to 0.13, making the original demo look primitive
The new movie aims to counter the misconception that Rails is only about Scaffolding
The screencast premiered at the FISL 6.0 conference in Brazil, showing early international Rails adoption
money_quotes: The original Rails movie left jaws hanging and had developers all over the world stepping through it in slow motion to take it all in.
Rails 0.5 looks almost primitive from the eyes of a developer working with Rails 0.13.
It's 50% longer, but shows at least 150% more.
key_insight: The rapid evolution of Rails from 0.5 to 0.13 warranted a new demo to showcase how far the framework had come beyond its initial Scaffolding-focused impression.
